glosses: to talk bullshit or ramble nonsense
to disregard something, as in 'fuck it'
to fuck or mess around with something or someone

examples.polish: On znowu pierdoli o polityce.
examples.literalEnglish: He again fucks about politics.
examples.naturalEnglish: He's rambling crap about politics again.

examples.polish: Ja to pierdolę, idę do domu.
examples.literalEnglish: I this fuck, I am going home.
examples.naturalEnglish: Fuck this, I'm going home.

notes: Highly polysemous vulgar root. The intended meaning depends strongly on syntax, object choice, and tone. The sample glosses focus on common colloquial uses rather than exhausting the full semantic range.

glosses: to add a lot more; pile on excessively
to land a hard verbal or physical hit

examples.polish: Nie dopierdol jeszcze więcej chilli, bo nikt tego nie zje.
examples.literalEnglish: Do not add-fuck still more chilli, because nobody this will-eat.
examples.naturalEnglish: Don't dump in even more chilli or nobody will eat it.

examples.polish: Na końcu dopierdolił mu tekstem o spóźnieniach.
examples.literalEnglish: At the end he hit him with a line about latenesses.
examples.naturalEnglish: At the end he really laid into him about being late.

children.notes: Often suggests excess, final force, or an emphatic last move. In perfective use, the non-past forms usually refer to the future.

glosses: to keep piling more onto something
to keep laying into someone

examples.polish: Szef cały tydzień dopierdala nam nowe poprawki.
examples.literalEnglish: The boss all week keeps-adding-fuck to-us new corrections.
examples.naturalEnglish: The boss has been piling new revisions on us all week.

children.notes: Secondary imperfective built on the perfective partner dopierdolić.

glosses: to slam or hit hard
to attach or place something roughly
to throw a cutting remark at someone

examples.polish: Przypierdolił drzwiami tak mocno, że wypadła szyba.
examples.literalEnglish: He hit-with the door so hard that fell-out the pane.
examples.naturalEnglish: He slammed the door so hard the pane popped out.

children.notes: A productive slang form with physical and metaphorical uses. The specific reading depends on the object or complement.

glosses: to pick on someone
to nitpick or hassle someone over details

examples.polish: Nie przypierdalaj się do każdego przecinka.
examples.literalEnglish: Do not pick-fuck-yourself to every comma.
examples.naturalEnglish: Stop nitpicking every comma.

children.notes: Lexicalized reflexive use. The reflexive marker carries an idiomatic interpersonal meaning rather than literal self-action.

glosses: to smash something to pieces
to scatter things all over
to blow someone's mind in slangy positive use

examples.polish: Jednym ruchem rozpierdolił stary monitor.
examples.literalEnglish: With one move he smashed-fuck the old monitor.
examples.naturalEnglish: With one move he smashed the old monitor.

examples.polish: Dzieci rozpierdoliły zabawki po całym pokoju.
examples.literalEnglish: The children scattered-fuck the toys across the whole room.
examples.naturalEnglish: The kids scattered the toys all over the room.

children.notes: Very strong slang item. The force can be literal destruction, complete disorder, or emphatic impact.

glosses: to keep tearing apart or wrecking
to keep spreading something everywhere

examples.polish: Ten bas rozpierdala mi głośniki.
examples.literalEnglish: This bass keeps-wrecking-fuck my speakers.
examples.naturalEnglish: That bass is blowing my speakers apart.

children.notes: Secondary imperfective with ongoing or repeated-event meaning.

glosses: to crash badly
to sprawl or collapse heavily

examples.polish: Rozpierdolił się na rowerze na mokrym zakręcie.
examples.literalEnglish: He crashed-fuck-himself on the bike on the wet bend.
examples.naturalEnglish: He wiped out on the bike on the wet curve.

children.notes: Often used for spectacular falls or crashes, literal or metaphorical.

glosses: to screw something up
to run off or disappear quickly
to send someone away rudely in certain constructions

examples.polish: Spierdoliłem termin i klient się wkurzył.
examples.literalEnglish: I screwed-fuck the deadline and the client got angry.
examples.naturalEnglish: I fucked up the deadline and the client got angry.

examples.polish: Po kolacji po prostu spierdolił z imprezy.
examples.literalEnglish: After dinner he simply ran-fuck from the party.
examples.naturalEnglish: After dinner he just bailed from the party.

children.notes: A strong colloquial item with clearly different readings depending on transitivity and surrounding syntax.

glosses: to be running off
to be screwing things up repeatedly

examples.polish: Jak usłyszeli syreny, zaczęli spierdalać.
examples.literalEnglish: When they heard sirens, they started running-fuck-away.
examples.naturalEnglish: When they heard the sirens, they started running off.

children.notes: Often used as a harsh motion verb meaning to get out fast.

glosses: to break down
to go badly wrong

examples.polish: System spierdolił się po aktualizacji.
examples.literalEnglish: The system broke-fuck-itself after the update.
examples.naturalEnglish: The system broke after the update.

children.notes: Common for software, machinery, or plans that fail abruptly.

glosses: to shove or put something into something else forcefully
to eat something greedily
to slam something into an obstacle

examples.polish: On potrafi wpierdolić pół blachy sernika sam.
examples.literalEnglish: He can eat-fuck half a tray of cheesecake alone.
examples.naturalEnglish: He can demolish half a tray of cheesecake on his own.

examples.polish: Kierowca wpierdolił auto w barierkę.
examples.literalEnglish: The driver shoved-fuck the car into the barrier.
examples.naturalEnglish: The driver rammed the car into the guardrail.

children.notes: Another highly contextual form. Object choice often makes the intended meaning obvious: food, insertion, or collision.

glosses: to be stuffing oneself or eating noisily
to keep shoving something in

examples.polish: Cały dzień wpierdala chipsy przed monitorem.
examples.literalEnglish: All day he eats-fuck chips in front of the monitor.
examples.naturalEnglish: He spends all day shoveling chips in front of the monitor.

children.notes: Imperfective partner emphasizing repeated or ongoing action.

glosses: to barge in where one should not
to crash into something

examples.polish: Wpierdolili się bez kolejki do gabinetu.
examples.literalEnglish: They barged-fuck-themselves without queue into the office.
examples.naturalEnglish: They barged into the office without waiting their turn.

examples.polish: Rowerzysta wpierdolił się w kałużę.
examples.literalEnglish: The cyclist crashed-fuck-himself into a puddle.
examples.naturalEnglish: The cyclist crashed into a puddle.

children.notes: Reflexive form that can describe either intrusive social entry or literal impact into an object or place.

glosses: to hit repeatedly or very hard
to produce or play something very loudly
to pile up or do a great deal of something

examples.polish: Napierdolił go kilka razy, zanim ktoś ich rozdzielił.
examples.literalEnglish: He hit-fuck him several times before somebody separated them.
examples.naturalEnglish: He hit him several times before somebody pulled them apart.

examples.polish: Sąsiad od rana napierdolił muzyką tak głośno, że nie dało się spać.
examples.literalEnglish: The neighbour since morning blasted-fuck with music so loudly that it was not possible to sleep.
examples.naturalEnglish: The neighbour blasted music so loudly all morning that it was impossible to sleep.

examples.polish: W weekend napierdoliłem tyle kilometrów, że ledwo czuję nogi.
examples.literalEnglish: At the weekend I did-fuck so many kilometres that I barely feel my legs.
examples.naturalEnglish: I covered so many kilometres over the weekend that I can barely feel my legs.

children.notes: Strongly emphatic form often implying large quantity, repeated impact, or excessive intensity. Common in contexts of fighting, noise, work, motion, or physical output.

glosses: to keep hitting repeatedly
to be blasting loudly
to keep doing something intensely or at high speed

examples.polish: Ktoś cały czas napierdala w kaloryfer na klatce.
examples.literalEnglish: Somebody all the time keeps-hitting-fuck at the radiator in the stairwell.
examples.naturalEnglish: Someone keeps banging on the radiator in the stairwell.

examples.polish: Deszcz napierdalał w szyby przez całą noc.
examples.literalEnglish: The rain kept-hitting-fuck at the windows through the whole night.
examples.naturalEnglish: The rain kept hammering against the windows all night.

examples.polish: Od miesiąca napierdalam nad tym projektem bez przerwy.
examples.literalEnglish: For a month I keep-working-fuck over this project without a break.
examples.naturalEnglish: I've been grinding away at this project nonstop for a month.

children.notes: Secondary imperfective with ongoing, repeated, or habitual force. Very productive in colloquial speech, including metaphorical uses for work, noise, weather, and movement.

glosses: to get very drunk
to crash into each other or get into a fight in some contexts

examples.polish: Na weselu napierdolili się już przed północą.
examples.literalEnglish: At the wedding they got-fuck-themselves already before midnight.
examples.naturalEnglish: They got completely smashed at the wedding before midnight.

examples.polish: Po meczu dwie grupy napierdoliły się pod stadionem.
examples.literalEnglish: After the match two groups hit-fuck-themselves under the stadium.
examples.naturalEnglish: After the match two groups got into a fight outside the stadium.

children.notes: Often means to get heavily intoxicated, especially with alcohol. In some contexts it can also indicate violent mutual confrontation.

glosses: to be fighting with each other
to keep getting wasted or drunk in a repeated way

examples.polish: Znowu napierdalają się na parkingu przez jakieś głupoty.
examples.literalEnglish: Again they are hitting-fuck-themselves in the car park over some stupid things.
examples.naturalEnglish: They're fighting in the car park again over some stupid nonsense.

examples.polish: Cały weekend napierdalali się w akademiku zamiast odpocząć.
examples.literalEnglish: The whole weekend they were getting-fuck-themselves in the dorm instead of resting.
examples.naturalEnglish: They spent the whole weekend getting wasted in the dorm instead of resting.

children.notes: Imperfective reflexive form with reciprocal or repeated-event meaning. Most often used for fighting, but can also refer to repeated heavy drinking in slangy contexts.

glosses: to fuck something up
to talk nonsense briefly
to mix something up / confuse things

examples.polish: Popierdoliłem ten kod i teraz nic nie działa.
examples.literalEnglish: I-fucked-up this code and now nothing works.
examples.naturalEnglish: I fucked up the code and now nothing works.

examples.polish: On coś popierdolił na spotkaniu o budżecie.
examples.literalEnglish: He something fucked-up/rambled at the meeting about the budget.
examples.naturalEnglish: He was talking nonsense about the budget in the meeting.

children.notes: Perfective verb indicating a completed episode, often with a negative result. The prefix 'po-' suggests a brief, bounded action or a single instance of messing up, talking nonsense, or confusing things.

glosses: to go to shit (internally / mentally)
to lose one's sense / lose the plot
to become messed up / disordered

examples.polish: Wielu ludziom w główkach się popierdoliło.
examples.literalEnglish: To-many people in little-heads itself fucked-up.
examples.naturalEnglish: A lot of people have lost the plot.

examples.polish: Coś mi się popierdoliło z tymi datami.
examples.literalEnglish: Something to-me itself fucked-up with these dates.
examples.naturalEnglish: I got the dates mixed up.

children.notes: Reflexive form indicating a completed negative change of state, often mental, cognitive, or systemic. Commonly used with a dative experiencer (e.g. 'mi', 'mu', 'ludziom') to mean that something has gone wrong internally rather than being actively caused. The prefix 'po-' contributes a bounded, completed event, often with an implicit sense of deterioration or breakdown.

glosses: to throw something out forcefully
to fire or kick someone out
to make something suddenly jut out or shoot outward in some contexts

examples.polish: Wypierdolił stare krzesło na śmietnik bez zastanowienia.
examples.literalEnglish: He threw-fuck the old chair onto the rubbish without thinking.
examples.naturalEnglish: He chucked the old chair out onto the rubbish heap without thinking.

examples.polish: Szef go wypierdolił po trzecim spóźnieniu.
examples.literalEnglish: The boss threw-fuck him out after the third lateness.
examples.naturalEnglish: The boss fired him after the third time he was late.

children.notes: Usually implies forceful expulsion outward, whether of an object, a person, or something metaphorically removed from a place or role.

glosses: to keep throwing things out
to keep kicking someone out
to fuck off or get out in imperative and motion uses

examples.polish: Wypierdalaj z mojego pokoju.
examples.literalEnglish: Throw-fuck-yourself-out from my room.
examples.naturalEnglish: Get the fuck out of my room.

examples.polish: Co tydzień wypierdalam stare kartony z piwnicy.
examples.literalEnglish: Every week I keep-throwing-fuck old boxes from the basement.
examples.naturalEnglish: Every week I throw old boxes out of the basement.

children.notes: Very common as a harsh imperative telling someone to leave. Outside that, it keeps the ongoing or repeated sense of expulsion.

glosses: to fall over badly
to crash violently

examples.polish: Wypierdolił się na lodzie pod sklepem.
examples.literalEnglish: He fell-fuck-himself on the ice under the shop.
examples.naturalEnglish: He went arse over tit on the ice outside the shop.

examples.polish: Motocyklista wypierdolił się na zakręcie.
examples.literalEnglish: The motorcyclist crashed-fuck-himself on the bend.
examples.naturalEnglish: The motorcyclist crashed on the bend.

children.notes: A very common reflexive crash/fall verb in colloquial speech, often for sudden and messy loss of balance or control.

glosses: to keep falling over
to keep crashing or wiping out

examples.polish: Na tych schodach ludzie ciągle się wypierdalają.
examples.literalEnglish: On these stairs people keep falling-fuck-themselves.
examples.naturalEnglish: People keep falling over on those stairs.

children.notes: Imperfective reflexive partner with repeated-event meaning.

glosses: to steal something
to hit very hard
to drive, throw, or move something very fast in some contexts

examples.polish: Ktoś mi zapierdolił rower spod klatki.
examples.literalEnglish: Someone stole-fuck me the bike from under the stairwell.
examples.naturalEnglish: Someone nicked my bike from outside the building.

examples.polish: Jak mu zapierdolę, to się nauczy.
examples.literalEnglish: If I hit-fuck him, then he will learn.
examples.naturalEnglish: If I smack him, he'll learn.

children.notes: One of the common high-intensity colloquial forms. Theft is a very salient reading, but force and speed also appear depending on the object and syntax.

glosses: to work very hard
to move very fast
to keep doing something with intense effort

examples.polish: Cały tydzień zapierdalam po dwanaście godzin dziennie.
examples.literalEnglish: The whole week I keep-working-fuck for twelve hours daily.
examples.naturalEnglish: I've been working my arse off twelve hours a day all week.

examples.polish: Ten pociąg zapierdala przez pół kraju bez postoju.
examples.literalEnglish: This train speeds-fuck through half the country without a stop.
examples.naturalEnglish: That train tears across half the country without stopping.

children.notes: Very common for strenuous work or very rapid motion. Strong, vulgar, but highly productive in everyday colloquial speech.

glosses: to get crammed full or blocked up
to crash into something in some contexts

examples.polish: Kibel się zapierdolił i znowu trzeba dzwonić po hydraulika.
examples.literalEnglish: The toilet blocked-fuck-itself and again one must call for the plumber.
examples.naturalEnglish: The toilet got completely blocked up again, so we need to call the plumber.

examples.polish: Na śliskim asfalcie zapierdolił się w barierkę.
examples.literalEnglish: On the slippery asphalt he crashed-fuck-himself into the barrier.
examples.naturalEnglish: On the slippery road he slammed into the barrier.

children.notes: The blocked-up reading is especially common with pipes, toilets, and other things that jam or clog. Impact readings occur too, but are more context-dependent.

glosses: to keep working oneself ragged
to keep getting clogged up or jammed

examples.polish: Nie będę się tak zapierdalać za te pieniądze.
examples.literalEnglish: I will not keep-working-fuck-myself like this for that money.
examples.naturalEnglish: I'm not going to work myself into the ground for that money.

children.notes: This reflexive imperfective often shades toward self-exhaustion or burdensome overexertion.

glosses: to scold someone harshly
to eat something up quickly

examples.polish: Szef mnie opierdolił przy całym zespole.
examples.literalEnglish: The boss scolded-fuck me in front of the whole team.
examples.naturalEnglish: The boss tore me off a strip in front of the whole team.

examples.polish: On sam opierdolił całą pizzę w dziesięć minut.
examples.literalEnglish: He himself ate-fuck the whole pizza in ten minutes.
examples.naturalEnglish: He demolished the whole pizza by himself in ten minutes.

children.notes: A common slang form, especially for getting told off. The food reading is also widespread in colloquial use.

glosses: to keep scolding or chewing someone out
to keep eating something greedily

examples.polish: Ojciec codziennie opierdala go za bałagan.
examples.literalEnglish: The father every day keeps-scolding-fuck him for the mess.
examples.naturalEnglish: His dad chews him out every day over the mess.

examples.polish: Siedzi i opierdala chipsy przed serialem.
examples.literalEnglish: He sits and keeps-eating-fuck crisps in front of the series.
examples.naturalEnglish: He's sat there scoffing crisps in front of the telly.

children.notes: Straightforward imperfective partner for repeated scolding or habitual greedy eating.

glosses: to slack off
to mess about instead of working

examples.polish: Przestań się opierdalać i kończ raport.
examples.literalEnglish: Stop slacking-fuck-yourself and finish the report.
examples.naturalEnglish: Stop arsing about and finish the report.

children.notes: Highly common lexicalized reflexive meaning to shirk effort or avoid getting on with a task.
